articles.title: MIDJOURNEY LAUNCHES ITS WEBSITE AND BECOMES FREE TO USE
articles.summary: Midjourney has launched a website, making its image generation capabilities accessible to all with a free trial of 25 AI-generated images. New users can register using a Google or Discord account, simplifying access without needing a separate login. Midjourney has provided a tutorial video to help users maximize their free image quota. Continued use after the trial requires a monthly subscription, with plans starting at $10.

articles.title: SAMSUNG ODYSSEY 3D OFFERS A GLASSES-FREE 3D EXPERIENCE FOR GAMERS AND CREATORS
articles.summary: The Samsung Odyssey 3D monitor introduces a breakthrough in stereoscopic 3D technology by enabling 3D viewing without the need for glasses or headsets. It utilizes built-in stereo cameras to track eye movements and a lenticular lens to display separate images to each eye, creating a floating 3D effect. Primarily aimed at gamers, the monitor features 4K resolution, 1 ms response time, and a 165Hz refresh rate, making it valuable for designers and content creators working in 3D spaces.

articles.title: IDEOGRAM 2.0 HAS ARRIVED TO EAT MIDJOURNEY'S LUNCH
articles.summary: The competition for AI image generation is intensifying, with Ideogram announcing the release of Ideogram 2.0. The updated version features five distinct image styles: General, Realistic, Design, 3D, and Anime. In addition to new creative capabilities, Ideogram 2.0 has introduced an iOS app, a beta version of its API, and a visual search engine. Subscription plans range from a generous free tier to a $48/month Pro plan, offering varying levels of image generation credits.

articles.title: PUTTING DESIGN AT THE HEART OF TECHNOLOGY
articles.summary: Design projects integrate branding, technology, and user experience, which is essential to creating a successful product. Prioritizing simplicity and consistency in design systems enhances customer satisfaction and brand identity. By developing adaptable frameworks, businesses can ensure efficient collaboration, faster deployment, and a unified brand presence.

articles.title: GOOGLE'S AI ‘REIMAGINE' TOOL HELPED US ADD WRECKS, DISASTERS, AND CORPSES TO OUR PHOTOS
articles.summary: Google's new Pixel 9 tool, "Reimagine," allows users to easily generate AI-modified images by typing prompts, raising concerns about potential misuse. While Google claims it has guardrails in place, the ability to create disturbing and misleading content is already outpacing the tools available to detect or prevent its spread, raising the need for more skepticism toward online images.

articles.title: HOW WE MADE OUR HARDWARE PACKAGING 100% PLASTIC-FREE
articles.summary: Google has achieved its goal of making all packaging for Pixel, Fitbit, and Nest devices 100% plastic-free ahead of its 2025 target. Its new packaging uses innovative materials such as more robust, stretchable paper and molded fiber pulp from recycled newspapers. These materials reduce plastic waste and enhance recyclability and visual appeal. Google has shared its updated Plastic-Free Packaging Design Guide to help other companies transition to sustainable packaging solutions.

articles.title: CIRCULAR TEXT (FIGMA PLUGIN)
articles.summary: A Figma plugin that generates circular and arc text.

articles.title: ICONS FOR AI
articles.summary: This collection features over 100 free 16x16 SVG icons tailored for AI use cases under a CC BY 4.0 license.

articles.title: INFOGRAPHICS ON FILM, TELEVISION, AND SPORTS
articles.summary: Visu features infographics highlighting key moments in film, television, and sports.

articles.title: KIT STUDIO'S RETAIL IDENTITY FOR LONDON TRANSPORT MUSEUM TAPS INTO TFL'S GRAPHIC ICONOGRAPHY
articles.summary: Kit Studio developed a new retail identity and packaging for the London Transport Museum, drawing from TFL's graphic iconography and visual history. The design features a sustainable system of colored 'tiles' that balances the brand's heritage with a fresh, modern approach, reducing plastic use and utilizing a limited color palette and Johnston typeface.

articles.title: ROBOT FOOD GIVES GERMAN NUTRITION BRAND A VIBRANT NEW LOOK AND FEEL
articles.summary: Leeds-based agency Robot Food redesigned the German health-food brand More, focusing on joy and self-empowerment with a vibrant color palette and expressive 'M' logo. The rebrand aimed to shift consumer perceptions of healthy food through positive messaging and appealing product photography.

articles.title: HOW TO DEAL WITH AN ENGINEERING TEAM THAT IS OUTPACING YOUR DESIGNS
articles.summary: Adopting "Slow Productivity" can help maintain quality when facing pressure from an engineering team outpacing your designs. Focus on fewer tasks, work naturally, and leverage AI tools for tedious jobs. This approach ensures thoughtful design progress without sacrificing quality, even under tight deadlines and high expectations.

articles.title: BEHIND THE DESIGN: CRAFTING OVER 800+ ICONS FOR WEBFLOW'S REBRAND
articles.summary: Webflow's rebrand involved designing over 800 icons with a focus on simplicity, consistency, and pixel perfection.

articles.title: CREATIVE BLOCK? A WORK-FRIENDLY BEER TO GET THINGS FLOWING AGAIN
articles.summary: London-based agency Office of Overview created "Creative Juice," a low-alcohol beer designed to boost creativity during work hours.

articles.title: GENERATIVE AI IS SLIDING INTO THE ‘TROUGH OF DISILLUSIONMENT'
articles.summary: Generative AI is entering the "trough of disillusionment" as companies focus on tangible ROI and excitement fades.
